title: The Dong Du Movement
summary: The journey of patriotic youth to Japan to learn new knowledge to advance the country.

slides.text: To defeat the French invaders, Phan Boi Chau believed that we needed to learn new things from advanced countries, especially Japan.
slides.imagePrompt: A boat heading east towards a rising sun over the sea, people on the boat looking excited, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: He organized the 'Dong Du' (meaning 'Travel East') movement - taking hundreds of Vietnamese youth to Japan to learn both knowledge and combat tactics.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese students in traditional hats combined with modern umbrellas in a Japanese street with cherry blossoms, fusion of cultures, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Despite a very hard life in Japan, the young men studied diligently and underwent military training with the burning desire to return and save the country.
slides.imagePrompt: Students studying science and military tactics in a classroom, drawings of machines and maps, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The Dong Du movement is a wonderful testament to our people's spirit of learning and thirst for innovation in the early 20th century.
slides.imagePrompt: Symbolic image of a book and a torch lighting the way across the sea, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which country did the 'Dong Du' movement send Vietnamese youth to study in?
What was the main goal of the Dong Du movement?
options: To learn new knowledge to save the country
slides.answer: To learn new knowledge to save the country
